heading: Edit One Document
body:
To modify a document, hover over the document and click the pencil
icon:

![Document Edit Selection in Table View](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/table-view-modify.png)

After you click the pencil icon, the document enters edit mode.

When you edit a document in List or Table view, Atlas performs a
[findOneAndUpdate](manual/reference/method/db.collection.findOneAndUpdate)
operation and updates only those fields that you have
changed.

If Atlas detects that you have changed fields
that were modified outside of Atlas, it notifies you, preventing
you from accidentally overwriting the changes made outside of Atlas.
You can choose to proceed and replace the document by clicking
Replace, or cancel your changes.

To delete a field from a document:

- Click the value of the field you want to delete.

- Click the  icon.

- Click Update to confirm your changes.

To add a new field to the document:

- Click the field after which you wish to add the new field.

- Click the  icon.

- Click Add Field after \<Field Name>.

- Populate your newly created field.

- Click Update to confirm your changes.

While modifying a document, you have the option to revert changes
made to a field prior to saving the modified document.

Click the revert icon which appears on the
right side of the edited table element.

![Document Revert Changes in Table View](/_includes/images/atlas-ui/compass/revert-doc-table-view.png)
